title: geum caryophyllatum
intro: Geum caryophyllatum, also known as the white avens or creeping avens, is a perennial plant in the rose family native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of geum caryophyllatum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
